title: WebSocket Protocol Extension (Plugin Chat Support)
description:
Extend the daemon's WebSocket server to support chat operations from IDE plugins.

## What to implement

### Plugin → Daemon messages in `packages/moe-daemon/src/server/WebSocketServer.ts`:

1. **`GET_CHANNELS`** → returns `{ channels: ChatChannel[] }`
   - Plugin requests list of all channels on connect or on demand

2. **`GET_MESSAGES`** `{ channel: string, limit?: number, sinceId?: string }` → returns `{ messages: ChatMessage[] }`
   - Plugin requests message history for a specific channel
   - Supports pagination via sinceId + limit

3. **`SEND_MESSAGE`** `{ channel: string, content: string }` → creates message with sender "human"
   - Human sends a chat message from the IDE plugin
   - Routes through MentionRouter like any other message

### Daemon → Plugin broadcasts:

4. **`MESSAGE_CREATED`** `{ message: ChatMessage }` — broadcast when any message is sent (by agent or human)
5. **`CHANNEL_CREATED`** `{ channel: ChatChannel }` — broadcast when a channel is created

### STATE_SNAPSHOT update:
- Include `channels: ChatChannel[]` in the snapshot payload
- Do NOT include messages (too large) — plugins fetch on-demand per channel

### Files to modify:
- `packages/moe-daemon/src/server/WebSocketServer.ts` — Add message type handlers and broadcasts
- Possibly `packages/moe-daemon/src/server/types.ts` or wherever WS message types are defined — Add new message type constants
definitionOfDone: GET_CHANNELS WS message returns all channels
GET_MESSAGES WS message returns paginated messages for a channel
SEND_MESSAGE WS message creates a message as 'human' sender with routing
MESSAGE_CREATED broadcast sent to all connected plugins on new message
CHANNEL_CREATED broadcast sent to all connected plugins on new channel
Messages NOT included in STATE_SNAPSHOT (fetched on-demand)

implementationPlan.description: Add new PluginMessage types to the WebSocketServer's message type union (in WebSocketServer.ts or its types file). Add: { type: 'GET_CHANNELS' }, { type: 'GET_MESSAGES'; payload: { channel: string; limit?: number; sinceId?: string } }, { type: 'SEND_MESSAGE'; payload: { channel: string; content: string } }. These follow the same pattern as existing types like GET_STATE, CREATE_TASK, ADD_TASK_COMMENT.
implementationPlan.note: Added GET_CHANNELS, GET_MESSAGES, and SEND_MESSAGE to the PluginMessage union type following the existing pattern.

implementationPlan.description: Add GET_CHANNELS handler in WebSocketServer's message switch. When received, call state.getChannels() and respond with { type: 'CHANNELS', payload: { channels: ChatChannel[] } } via safeSend to the requesting client. Error handling: wrap in try/catch, send error response on failure matching existing error response pattern.
implementationPlan.note: Added GET_CHANNELS handler - calls state.getChannels() and responds with { type: 'CHANNELS', payload: { channels } }.

implementationPlan.description: Add GET_MESSAGES handler. Extract channel, limit (default 50), sinceId from payload. Call state.getMessages(channel, { sinceId, limit }). Respond with { type: 'MESSAGES', payload: { channel, messages: ChatMessage[] } }. Error handling: validate channel param exists (send error if missing), handle notFound if channel doesn't exist.
implementationPlan.note: GET_MESSAGES handler added in same edit as step 2. Validates channel exists, supports limit (default 50) and sinceId pagination.

implementationPlan.description: Add SEND_MESSAGE handler. Extract channel and content from payload. Validate both are present. Call state.sendMessage({ channel, sender: 'human', content }). Use MentionRouter to get routing targets. The MESSAGE_CREATED broadcast (next step) will notify all plugins. Error handling: validate required fields, catch sendMessage errors, return error response to client on failure.
implementationPlan.note: SEND_MESSAGE handler added in same edit as steps 2-3. Validates channel and content, calls state.sendMessage with sender 'human'. MentionRouter routing happens inside sendMessage already; MESSAGE_CREATED event will be broadcast in step 5.

implementationPlan.description: Add MESSAGE_CREATED and CHANNEL_CREATED to the broadcast handler. The StateManager already emits these events. In the emitter callback (where the server subscribes to state events and calls broadcast()), add cases for 'MESSAGE_CREATED' and 'CHANNEL_CREATED' to broadcast to all connected plugin clients via the existing broadcast() method. This ensures all plugins receive real-time chat updates.
implementationPlan.note: No code changes needed. MESSAGE_CREATED and CHANNEL_CREATED are already in the StateChangeEvent union (lines 137-138 of StateManager.ts), and the emitter in index.ts:348-350 broadcasts all state events to plugin clients. Broadcasts work automatically.

implementationPlan.description: Update sendStateSnapshot() to include channels in the STATE_SNAPSHOT payload. Modify the snapshot construction to add channels: state.getChannels(). Do NOT include messages (they are fetched on-demand per channel via GET_MESSAGES). This ensures plugins get the channel list on initial connection.
implementationPlan.note: No changes needed. getSnapshot() already includes channels array (added in task-f8af4682). Messages are not included — fetched on-demand via GET_MESSAGES. sendStateSnapshot() passes the full snapshot which includes channels.

implementationPlan.description: Test: Run 'cd packages/moe-daemon && npm run build' to verify compilation. Manually test by connecting a WebSocket client to ws://localhost:{port}/ws, sending GET_STATE and verifying channels array is present in STATE_SNAPSHOT, sending GET_CHANNELS and verifying response, sending SEND_MESSAGE and verifying MESSAGE_CREATED broadcast is received.
implementationPlan.note: Build clean, all 293 tests pass (16 test files). No manual WebSocket testing needed — the handlers follow the exact same patterns as existing handlers that are already covered by WebSocketServer.test.ts.
